LAW AND MOTION 302, RE: DOLLARDAYS INTERNATIONAL, INC., A DELAWARE, & JAMES JACOBS' MOTION FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES. NO APPEARANCE WAS MADE BY ANY PARTY, COUNSEL, OR INTERESTED PERSON. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PETITIONERS' "MOTION FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES" IS ORDERED STAYED UNTIL SUCH TIME AS EITHER PARTY MOVES FOR FEES AS TO REVIEW 2 OR BOTH SIDES DEFINITIVELY STATE THAT THEY WILL NOT DO SO. THE COURT DECLINES TO ADDRESS FEE ISSUES PIECEMEAL. (SEE OTO, L.L.C. V. KHO (2019) 8 CAL.5TH 111, 141.) COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR. CLERK: ROBERT WOODS. NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)

PRO HAC VICE RENEWAL FEE STATUS FOR ATTORNEY RAEESABBAS MOHAMED
Off Calendar Dec-13-2023 Continued To Jan-24-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 206

Motion For Attorneys Fees
Off Calendar Oct-04-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

RE: DOLLARDAYS INTERNATIONAL, INC., A DELAWARE, & JAMES JACOBS' Motion For Attorney's Fees.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person.

The court adopts its tentative ruling. Petitioners' "motion for attorney's fees" is ordered stayed until such time as either party moves for fees as to Review 2 or both sides definitively state that they will not do so. The court declines to address fee issues piecemeal. (See OTO, L.L.C. v. Kho (2019) 8 Cal.5th 111, 141.)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (302/RBU)

NOTICE OF HEARING ON PETITIONERS DOLLARDAYS INTERNATIONAL, INC. AND JAMES JACOBS PETITION TO COMPEL GLASSDOOR, INC. TO RESPOND TO SUBPOENA FOR BUSINESS RECORDS IN ACTION PENDING OUTSIDE CALIFORNIA PURSUANT TO CCP 2029.600 AND FOR AWARD OF COSTS (TRANSFER FROM 9:30 A.M. LAW & MOTION CALENDAR)
Off Calendar Mar-20-2023 Continued To May-15-23 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Attys. KARL S. KRONENBERGER, Esq. and LEAH ROSA VULIC, Esq. of Kronenberger Rosenfeld, LLP (Tel: 415-955-1155) and RAEESABBAS MOHAMED, Esq. of RM Warner, PLC (Tel: 480-331-9397) for petitioners;
Atty. WILLIAM J. FRIMEL, Esq. of Seubert French Frimel & Warner LLP (Tel: 650-322-3048) for respondent Glassdoor, Inc.

HEARING RE: PETITIONERS DOLLARDAYS INTERNATIONAL, INC. AND JAMES JACOBS' PETITION TO COMPEL GLASSDOOR, INC. TO RESPOND TO SUBPOENA FOR BUSINESS RECORDS IN ACTION PENDING OUTSIDE CALIFORNIA PURSUANT TO CCP 2029.600 AND FOR AWARD OF COSTS.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Hearing of the "petition to compel respondent Glassdoor, Inc. to respond to subpoena for business records in action pending outside California" is continued to May 15, 2023. This discovery dispute may be resolved by that time and further use of this California court in an out-of-state action may thus be unnecessary.

Glassdoor's website allows anonymous reviews of employers. Petitioners filed an Arizona action alleging that three Glassdoor reviews about them are defamatory. Petitioners' subpoena is designed to learn the reviewers' identities. (Memo. 1:3-21.) Petitioners have already learned the identity of two of the reviews' author without a compelled response to the subpoena and may be able to identify the author of the third review.

Counsel for respondent to prepare and submit a proposed order to the court.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Justus Balentine, CSR No. 13859, Email: justusbalentinecsr@gmail.com, Tel: 714-471-2597 (302/RBU)

NOTICE OF HEARING ON PETITIONERS DOLLARDAYS INTERNATIONAL, INC. AND JAMES JACOBS PETITION TO COMPEL GLASSDOOR, INC. TO RESPOND TO SUBPOENA FOR BUSINESS RECORDS IN ACTION PENDING OUTSIDE CALIFORNIA PURSUANT TO CCP 2029.600 AND FOR AWARD OF COSTS (TRANSFER FROM 9:30 A.M. LAW & MOTION CALENDAR)
Off Calendar Mar-02-2023 Continued To Mar-20-23 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

NOTICE OF HEARING ON PETITIONERS DOLLARDAYS INTERNATIONAL, INC. AND JAMES JACOBS PETITION TO COMPEL GLASSDOOR, INC. TO RESPOND TO SUBPOENA FOR BUSINESS RECORDS IN ACTION PENDING OUTSIDE CALIFORNIA PURSUANT TO CCP 2029.600 AND FOR AWARD OF COSTS

In the joint statement ordered by the court on February 9, 2023, petitioners report that they have "executed a confidential settlement agreement." The parties appear to disagree over the settlement agreement's import. Thus, petitioners are ordered to provide a copy of the agreement by March 16, 2023 via hand delivery for the court's in camera review. Hearing of this matter is continued to March 20, 2023.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer: W. Trupek, Not reported (302/RBU)
